frameworks.name: neural variability quenching
frameworks.one_line: trial-by-trial variability is reduced after stimulus onset.

frameworks.name: alpha-beta band oscillations
frameworks.one_line: oscillatory power suppression is proposed as the mechanism of quenching.

frameworks.name: inter-trial phase coherence
frameworks.one_line: ITPC is contrasted as an alternative oscillatory signature.

core_question: Are stimulus-induced neural variability quenching and oscillatory power suppression manifestations of the same underlying phenomenon?
key_assumptions: trial-by-trial fluctuations and oscillatory power reflect comparable neural dynamics
MEG sensors recover band-limited cortical activity faithfully
between-subject variation in quenching is a stable individual trait
manipulations: repeated presentations of a visual stimulus
selective band-stop filtering (alpha-beta vs other bands)
key_findings: variability quenching and alpha-beta power suppression share timing, amplitude, and spatial topography
removing the alpha-beta band eliminates variability quenching while removing other bands does not
individual alpha-beta power magnitude explains 86% of between-subject differences in quenching
inter-trial phase coherence is not correlated with quenching
interpretations: variability quenching is largely driven by suppression of alpha-beta oscillatory power
amplitude rather than phase dynamics couples oscillations to trial-by-trial variability
